1.title: Open Floor Rebound Hammer Test Evaluation Panel

content.text: System shall open a right-side panel when the user clicks the Floor Rebound Hammer Test from the Rules and Action → Testing list.
the evaluation panel to open when I select the Floor Rebound Hammer Test
I can review the evaluation model.
I am viewing the Rules and Action → Testing list,
I click the Floor Rebound Hammer Test,
the system opens a right-side evaluation panel.
System shall display the panel title “Floor Rebound Hammer Test — Evaluation Model”.
the panel title displayed at the top
I know which evaluation model I am viewing.
the system displays the title “Floor Rebound Hammer Test — Evaluation Model”.
System shall display the Global scope label beside the test title.
the Global scope label displayed beside the test title
the system shows the Global scope label.

System shall display a description “In- situ concrete surface strength estimation using rebound hammer readings.” indicating that the test estimates in-situ concrete surface strength using rebound hammer readings.
I understand the purpose of the test.
the system displays the text indicating the test estimates in-situ concrete surface strength using rebound hammer readings.
System shall display the Measurement Method section explaining rebound hammer reading rules.
I understand the rebound hammer reading guidelines.
the system shows the Measurement Method section.
System shall require a minimum of 10 rebound readings per test zone.
the system to require a minimum number of rebound readings
rebound readings are recorded for a test zone,
it requires at least ten rebound readings.
System shall remove outlier readings where the absolute difference between Ri and the mean value exceeds 6.
the system to remove outlier readings
inaccurate measurements do not affect results.
it removes readings where the absolute difference between Ri and the mean exceeds 6.
System shall recompute the final mean rebound value after removing outliers.
the system to recompute the mean rebound value
final evaluation is based on valid readings.
it recalculates the final mean rebound value.
System shall display the Strength Calculation section.
I understand how compressive strength is derived.
the system shows the Strength Calculation section.
System shall determine the Final Mean Rebound value (Rf) from the processed rebound readings.
the system to determine the final mean rebound value
it can be used for strength calculation.
the system determines the Final Mean Rebound value (Rf).

11.title: Convert Rebound Value to Compressive Strength

content.text: System shall convert rebound value to using a calibration curve. –“Convert using Calibration curve.”
the system to convert rebound value to compressive strength
the structural strength can be evaluated.
the final mean rebound value is available,
the system converts the rebound value using a calibration curve.
System shall calculate test compressive strength using formula - “(fck,test) = f(Rf).”
the system to calculate the test compressive strength
the value can be compared with design strength.
it calculates fck,test using the function f(Rf).
System shall display a note indicating that “calibration curve stored in the system based on hammer type.”
I understand how strength conversion is performed.
the Strength Calculation section is displayed,
the system indicates that calibration curves are stored in the system based on hammer type.
System shall display the Strength Ratio section.
I understand how test strength is compared with design strength.
the system shows the Strength Ratio section.
System shall calculate Strength Ratio (SR) using the formula SR = fck,test / fck,design.
the system to calculate the Strength Ratio
I can determine compliance with design strength.
fck, test and fck,  design values are available,
it calculates SR = fck, test / fck, design.
System shall display the Acceptance Criteria section defining strength classification.
the system shows the Acceptance Criteria section.
System shall define Green condition when SR ≥ 1.00.
I know when concrete strength meets design requirements.
the system defines it as SR ≥ 1.00.
System shall define Amber condition when 0.85 ≤ SR < 1.00.
I know when concrete strength is slightly below design expectations.
the system defines it as 0.85 ≤ SR < 1.00.
System shall define Red condition when SR < 0.85.
I know when concrete strength is critically low.
the system defines it as SR < 0.85.
System shall display severity levels using colour indicators (Green, Amber, Red).
I can easily identify the severity classification.
the system shows color indicators for Green, Amber, and Red.
System shall display the System Defined label indicating the evaluation model is not editable.
I understand the evaluation model cannot be modified.
the system displays a “System Defined” label.
System shall provide a Close button to close the evaluation panel.
a Close button available in the panel
I can exit the evaluation view.
the system provides a Close button.
System shall provide a close (X) icon to close the panel.
a close (X) icon in the panel header
I can quickly dismiss the panel.
the system shows a close (X) icon in the top-right corner.

24.title: Return to Testing List After Closing Panel

content.text: System shall return the user to the Rules and Action → Testing list screen when the panel is closed.
the system to return me to the Rules and Action → Testing list after closing the panel
I can continue reviewing other tests.
I click the Close button or close (X) icon,
the system closes the panel and returns to the Rules and Action → Testing list screen.
